title: SLIP StreamDecoder: overflow check skips END delimiter, discarding subsequent valid packet
description:
File: python/src/lichen/slip/codec.py
Lines: 126-130

In StreamDecoder.feed(), when the buffer exceeds max_size:

    if len(self._buf) >= self._max_size:
        self._buf.clear()
        self._escaped = False
        self._overflow = True
        continue

The `continue` statement processes the next byte without checking if the current byte is END. If the byte that triggers overflow IS the END delimiter terminating the oversized packet, we:
1. Enter overflow mode (correct - discard oversized packet)
2. Skip the END delimiter entirely (BUG)
3. Discard all bytes until another END (BUG - next valid packet lost)

Example: Stream contains [2048 bytes of data][END][valid packet][END]
- When processing END after 2048 bytes, overflow triggers
- END is skipped, overflow mode entered
- All bytes of valid packet are discarded as overflow
- Second END clears overflow mode
- Result: valid packet is silently lost

Fix: Check if byte == END before entering overflow mode:

    if len(self._buf) >= self._max_size:
        self._buf.clear()
        self._escaped = False
        if byte != END:
            self._overflow = True
        continue
close_reason: Fixed END delimiter handling in overflow path; removed explanatory comments per style; test passes; commit 637d9947e
